content: Intrinsic curiosity signals from ICM/RND frameworks can be repurposed to dynamically adapt an agent’s working memory capacity allocation, prioritizing memory retention for surprising, under-explored state transitions that boost long-term reasoning while pruning redundant, predictable information that would otherwise cause capacity overload. This creates a self-regulating memory system where curiosity naturally shapes working memory constraints to align with reasoning demands.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
